architecture.summary: A processing pipeline for converting natural language household robot directions into executable simulation plans. The system uses SOMA (Socio-physical Model of Activities) as a shared ontological interface connecting: a natural language parser, a grounding module, reasoning steps that formulate simulation plans guided by human-computed data, and a virtual environment simulator. Symbolic knowledge from a central knowledge base (containing asserted facts, direction semantics, contextual knowledge, and annotated activity models) is retrieved and combined with sub-symbolic annotated models across all components.

biblio.title: Deep understanding of everyday activity commands for household robots

claims.statement: The pipeline includes a natural language parser and a module for natural language grounding, with several reasoning steps that formulate simulation plans in which robot actions are guided by data gathered using human computation

Employing an overarching ontological framework (SOMA) allows asserted facts to be stored alongside the semantics of directions, contextual knowledge, and annotated activity models in one central knowledge base
The unified knowledge base provides flexibility and reasoning capabilities as symbolic knowledge is combined with annotated sub-symbolic models
